samples.xml_description:
Implement an unsigned 16-bit multiplier using shift and accumulate operations. Clock signal. Active-low reset signal. Chip enable signal to initiate multiplication. Multiplicand input. Multiplier input. Product output. Completion flag indicating the end of multiplication. Shift count register. On every positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the negative edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the shift count register (i) is updated. if (rst_n == 0) Reset condition: Set shift count register (i) to 0. i = 0; if (start == 1 and i < 17) Start multiplication: Increment shift count register (i). i = i + 1; if (start == 0) Reset shift count register (i) if start signal is inactive. i = 0; On every positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the negative edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the multiplication completion flag (done_r) is updated. if (rst_n == 0) Reset condition: Set multiplication completion flag (done_r) to 0. done_r = 0; if (i == 16) Set multiplication completion flag (done_r) to 1 when shift count register (i) reaches 16. done_r = 1; if (i == 17) Reset multiplication completion flag (done_r) to 0 when shift count register (i) reaches 17. done_r = 0; On every positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the negative edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the module performs the shift and accumulate operation. if (rst_n == 0) Reset condition: Set multiplicand register (areg), multiplier register (breg), and product register (yout_r) to 0. areg = 0; breg = 0; yout_r = 0; if (start == 1) Start multiplication. if (i == 0) Store multiplicand (ain) and multiplier (bin) in registers. areg = ain; breg = bin; if (i > 0 and i < 17 and areg[i-1] == 1) Shift and accumulate: Accumulate the shifted multiplier (breg) in the product register (yout_r). yout_r = yout_r + (breg << (i-1) + 16');

Implement the design of an unsigned 16-bit multiplier using shift and accumulate operations.

Initialize the shift count register (i) to 0 on the positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the negative edge of the reset signal (rst_n).

Start multiplication when the start signal is active (start) and the shift count register (i) is less than 17. Increment the shift count register (i) by 1.

Reset the shift count register (i) to 0 when the start signal is inactive (!start).

Set the multiplication completion flag (done_r) to 1 when the shift count register (i) reaches 16, indicating the end of multiplication.

Reset the multiplication completion flag (done_r) to 0 when the shift count register (i) reaches 17.

On every positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the negative edge of the reset signal (rst_n), perform the shift and accumulate operation.

Store the multiplicand (ain) and multiplier (bin) in the multiplicand register (areg) and multiplier register (breg) when the shift count register (i) is 0.

Accumulate the shifted multiplier (breg) in the product register (yout_r) when the bit at position i-1 in the multiplicand register (areg) is high.

 Reset the shift count register (i) to 0. negative edge of rst_n Set i to 0. Increment the shift count register (i) by 1. positive edge of clk when start is 1 and i is less than 17 Increment i by 1. Reset the shift count register (i) to 0. negative edge of rst_n Set i to 0. Set the multiplication completion flag (done_r) to 1 when the shift count register (i) reaches 16. positive edge of clk when i is 16 Set done_r to 1. Reset the multiplication completion flag (done_r) to 0 when the shift count register (i) reaches 17. positive edge of clk when i is 17 Set done_r to 0. Store the multiplicand (ain) and multiplier (bin) in the multiplicand register (areg) and multiplier register (breg) when the shift count register (i) is 0. positive edge of clk when i is 0 Set areg to ain and breg to bin. Accumulate the shifted multiplier (breg) in the product register (yout_r) when the bit at position i-1 in the multiplicand register (areg) is high. positive edge of clk when i is greater than 0 and less than 17 and areg[i-1] is 1 Accumulate yout_r to yout_r + (breg << (i-1) + 16')

%Warning-WIDTH: multi_16bit.v:32:37: Operator SHIFTL expects 32 bits on the LHS, but LHS's VARREF 'a' generates 16 bits.
                                   : ... In instance multi_16bit
   32 |             product <= product + (a << (16 - count));
      |                                     ^~
                ... Use "/* verilator lint_off WIDTH */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
